title: About the Household Labour Force Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## About the Household Labour Force Survey
21 September 2023, 10:45am
Information about taking part in the Household Labour Force Survey, such as why we carry out the survey, how we selected you, and how to complete the questionnaire.
We carry out the Household Labour Force Survey (HLFS) to produce New Zealand’s official measure of employment. When you see employment figures in the media, they usually come from this survey.
The survey results also help to provide an accurate picture of New Zealand’s economy, which is useful for knowing where jobs need to be created and where training and education effort is needed.
Approximately fifteen thousand (15,000) households take part in this survey every three months. A house is selected using a fair statistical method to ensure the sample is an accurate representation of New Zealand. Every person aged 15 years or over living in a selected house needs to take part.
If you move away from a selected house, we will not contact you at your new address. You will be replaced by the new occupants of the selected house.
The questions included in this survey are being asked under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Our approach to survey compliance has more information about your legal obligation to participate in the Household Labour Force Survey.
Houses are selected into the survey for two years. Everyone aged 15 and over in the selected house will be interviewed once every three months.
Our face-to-face interview generally takes less than 10 minutes for each person. After that, we will contact you by phone to get a brief update on your employment situation. This call will take a few minutes and will be once every three months for up to two years. We do this to get an up-to-date and accurate picture of employment in New Zealand.
Once a year we will ask you some additional questions about your income. This will take about five minutes for most people.

## How to complete the survey
You’ll be interviewed by one of our field staff, who will fill in the survey questionnaire with the answers you give. The first interview is face-to-face, by an interviewer using a laptop. Subsequent interviews are by telephone.
- Employment and unemployment
